title: श्री शनि कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शनि कवचम् ॥

अस्य श्री शनैश्चरकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य कश्यप ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। शनैश्चरो देवता।
शनैश्चरप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
नीलाम्बरो नीलवपुः किरीटी गृध्रस्थितस्त्रासकरो धनुष्मान्।
चतुर्भुजः सूर्यसुतः प्रसन्नः सदा मम स्याद्वरदः प्रशान्तः॥

ॐ शनैश्चरः शिरः पातु ललाटं सूर्यनन्दनः।
नेत्रे छायात्मजः पातु कर्णौ पातु यमानुजः॥१॥

नासिकां वैवस्वतः पातु मुखं मे भास्करः सदा।
स्निग्धकण्ठश्च मे कण्ठं भुजौ पातु महाभुजः॥२॥

स्कन्धौ पातु शनिश्चैव करौ पातु शुभप्रदः।
वक्षः पातु यमभ्राता कुक्षिं पात्वसितस्तथा॥३॥

नाभिं ग्रहपतिः पातु मन्दः पातु कटिं तथा।
ऊरू ममान्तकः पातु याम्यो जानुयुगं तथा॥४॥

पादौ मन्दगतिः पातु सर्वाङ्गं पातु पिप्पलः।
अङ्गोपाङ्गानि सर्वाणि रक्षेन्मे सूर्यनन्दनः॥५॥

इत्येतत्कवचं दिव्यं यः पठेच्छृणुयादपि।
न तस्य जायते पीडा शनेर्नान्यग्रहस्य च॥६॥

त्रिकालपठनाद्धीमान् सर्वशत्रुविनाशनम्।
वन्ध्या पुत्रवती भूयाद्दरिद्रो धनवान् भवेत्॥७॥

रोगी रोगात्प्रमुच्येत बद्धो मुच्येत बन्धनात्।
शनैश्चरप्रसादेन सर्वाभीष्टमवाप्नुयात्॥८॥

॥ इति श्री शनि कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Śani Kavacham ||

Asya Shrī Śanaiścharakavachastotramantrasya Kaśyapa ṛṣiḥ.
Anuṣṭup chhandaḥ. Śanaiścharo devatā.
Śanaiścharaprītyarthe jape viniyogaḥ.

|| Dhyānam ||
Nīlāmbaro nīlavapuḥ kirīṭī gṛdhrasthitastrāsakaro dhanuṣmān.
Chaturbhujaḥ Sūryasutaḥ prasannaḥ sadā mama syādvaradaḥ praśāntaḥ.

Om Śanaiścharaḥ śiraḥ pātu lalāṭaṁ Sūryanandanaḥ.
Netre Chhāyātmajaḥ pātu karṇau pātu Yamānujaḥ.||1||

Nāsikāṁ Vaivasvataḥ pātu mukhaṁ me Bhāskaraḥ sadā.
Snigdhakaṇṭhaścha me kaṇṭhaṁ bhujau pātu Mahābhujaḥ.||2||

Skandhau pātu Śaniśchaiva karau pātu Śubhapradaḥ.
Vakṣaḥ pātu Yamabhrātā kukṣiṁ pātvasitastathā.||3||

Nābhiṁ Grahapatiḥ pātu Mandaḥ pātu kaṭiṁ tathā.
Ūrū mamāntakaḥ pātu Yāmyo jānuyugaṁ tathā.||4||

Pādau Mandagatiḥ pātu sarvāṅgaṁ pātu Pippalaḥ.
Aṅgopāṅgāni sarvāṇi rakṣenme Sūryanandanaḥ.||5||

Ityetatkavachaṁ divyaṁ yaḥ paṭhechchhṛṇuyādapi.
Na tasya jāyate pīḍā Śanernānyagrahasya cha.||6||

Trikālapaṭhanāddhīmān sarvaśatruvināśanam.
Vandhyā putravantī bhūyāddaridrō dhanavān bhavet.||7||

Rogī rogātpramuchyeta baddho muchyeta bandhanāt.
Śanaiścharaprasādena sarvābhīṣṭamavāpnuyāt.||8||

|| Iti Shrī Śani Kavachaṁ sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning: ध्यान: नीले वस्त्र और शरीर वाले, किरीटधारी, गृध्र (गिद्ध) पर विराजमान, भयकारी, धनुषधारी, चतुर्भुज, सूर्यपुत्र, प्रसन्न शनिदेव सदा मुझे वरदान दें। (1) शनैश्चर मेरे शिर की, सूर्यनन्दन ललाट की, छायात्मज नेत्रों की, यमानुज कानों की रक्षा करें। (2) वैवस्वत नासिका की, भास्कर मुख की, स्निग्धकण्ठ कण्ठ की, महाभुज भुजाओं की रक्षा करें। (3-5) शनि स्कन्धों की, शुभप्रद हाथों की, यमभ्राता वक्ष की, असित कुक्षि की, ग्रहपति नाभि की, मन्द कटि की, अन्तक ऊरुओं की, याम्य घुटनों की, मन्दगति पैरों की, पिप्पल सर्वांग की रक्षा करें। (6) इस दिव्य कवच को जो पढ़ता या सुनता है, उसे शनि और अन्य ग्रहों की पीड़ा नहीं होती। (7-8) तीनों काल पढ़ने से शत्रु नष्ट होते हैं, बाँझ स्त्री पुत्रवती होती है, दरिद्र धनवान बनता है, रोगी रोग से मुक्त होता है, बँधा हुआ बन्धन से मुक्त होता है।
benefits: Protects every body part from Saturn's malefic influence
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of afflictions of shani and other planets
Invokes children to the childless, wealth to the poor
Frees the sick from disease and the imprisoned from bondage
Three-time daily recitation destroys all enemies
Complete kavach (armor) prayer for total Shani protection
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Shani Dev, Shani Kavacham (श्री शनि कवचम्) stands as a divine armor prayer for protection. Drawn from Brahma Vaivarta Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to complete kavach (armor) prayer for total Shani protection and grant children to the childless, wealth to the poor. It is also said to protect every body part from Saturn's malefic influence. For best results, devotees chant this on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Shani Jayanti. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Shani Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Shani Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful armor prayer for protection from Saturn's malefic effects during Sade Sati and Dhaiya.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shani Kavacham and what does it protect against?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shani Kavacham is a Sanskrit kavacha (protective armour) prayer dedicated to Shani Dev — the planet Saturn, the sixth and slowest of the Vedic Navagraha, the karmic judge who delivers the fruits of past actions with strict impartiality. The kavacha encloses each limb of the chanter in Shani's protective grace, granting protection from Shani-dosha, the seven-and-a-half-year Sade Sati transit, the nineteen-year Shani Mahadasha, the dhaiya (two-and-a-half-year transit through the fourth and eighth houses from Moon), and the various afflictions Shani imparts when displeased: chronic disease, debt, unemployment, family separation, accidents, depression, paralysis and prolonged misfortune. It is preserved in the Brahma Vaivarta Purana and various Navagraha kavacha collections. (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Stotra Nidhi Shani Kavacham; Drikpanchang Navagraha.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Shani Dev in Hindu mythology?
seoFaqs.answer: Shani Dev is the divine son of Surya (the Sun) and his shadow-consort Chhaya, born dark-complexioned (in contrast to his radiant father) — a circumstance that prefigured his role as the cosmic dispenser of difficult karmic returns. The Brahma Vaivarta Purana, Skanda Purana and Markandeya Purana narrate how the infant Shani's gaze was so powerful that when he opened his eyes, his father Surya temporarily lost lustre, and many other deities suffered when subjected to Shani's drishti. He is the brother of Yama (god of death) and Yamuna (the river goddess), husband of Mandadevi, lord of Saturday, presiding deity of Shravana, Anuradha and Uttara Bhadrapada nakshatras, ruler of Capricorn and Aquarius. He is iconographically dark, four-armed, holding bow-arrow, sword and trident, riding a black crow or buffalo. (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Skanda Purana; Wikipedia Shani; Hindupedia Navagraha.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Shani not only a feared deity but also a bestower of supreme blessings?
seoFaqs.answer: Despite his reputation for severity, Shani is venerated as the Karma-phala-data (the giver of karmic fruits) who acts with absolute impartiality — he does not punish arbitrarily but only delivers what the soul has earned through its own actions. To the dharmic, virtuous and patient devotee, Shani becomes the supreme bestower of blessings: long-lasting wealth (since Shani's gifts endure unlike sudden gains from other planets), career stability and rise to high positions through patient effort, mastery in spiritual sadhana (Shani being the karaka of tapasya and detachment), longevity, and ultimately moksha. The famous Shani temple at Shingnapur in Maharashtra has no doors on any house in the village because Shani himself is regarded as the guardian — testimony to his protective grace. (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Shingnapur temple history; Hindupedia Shani upasana.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Shani Kavacham differ from Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach on this site?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shani Kavacham (this entry, from Brahma Vaivarta Purana) is the standard Navagraha-pattern kavacha following the head-to-feet limb-protection structure used for all nine grahas. The Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach (companion entry) is a more specialised and famous text drawn from the Shani Mahatmya in the Skanda Purana, structured as a vajra-panjara (diamond cage) that encloses the chanter in an impenetrable lattice of Shani's grace from all eight directions and the zenith and nadir. The Vajrapanjara is regarded as more powerful for severe affliction periods (Sade Sati peak, Mahadasha onset), while the standard kavacha is suited to daily protective recitation. Many devotees chant both during difficult Saturn periods. (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Shani Mahatmya Skanda Purana; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Shani Kavacham be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Saturday is the day prescribed for Shani across all Hindu astrological traditions, with brahma-muhurta (4-6 AM), sunset, and Rahukala (4:30-6 PM on Saturdays) being efficacious hours. Special days include Shani Jayanti (Jyeshtha Amavasya, May-June, the most important annual observance), Shani Trayodashi (when Trayodashi falls on Saturday), Shani Pradosham (Pradosham on Saturday), Amavasya, every Saturday during Sade Sati or Shani Mahadasha, and the eight-and-a-half year period collectively (Sade Sati 7.5 years + Ashtama Shani 1 year). Devotees suffering from Saturn affliction recite forty-day mandalas, ideally combined with Saturday vratas (oil offering, mustard-oil deepak, black sesame, donations to the poor and needy). (Sources: Drikpanchang Shani Jayanti; AstroSage Sade Sati; Hindupedia Shani upasana.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Shani Kavacham phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares the kavacha grants protection from all forms of Shani-dosha, mitigation of Sade Sati and Ashtama Shani severity, relief during Shani Mahadasha, freedom from chronic illness, paralysis, joint pain, neurological disorders and skin diseases that jyotisha attributes to Saturn affliction, removal of debt (rina-mochana), success in long-term endeavours requiring patience and persistence, career stability and elevation to positions of authority, longevity, and the cultivation of the Shani-virtues of patience, detachment, equanimity and spiritual maturity. Saturday recitation for forty consecutive days is held to manifest tangible relief. (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana phalashruti; Stotra Nidhi; AstroSage Shani remedies.)

seoFaqs.question: Where are the major Shani temples in India?
seoFaqs.answer: The most prominent Shani temples are: Shani Shingnapur in Ahmednagar district, Maharashtra (the most famous, where Shani is the swayambhu open-air black stone, no doors on village houses); Tirunallaru Shaneeswaran temple in Karaikal, Puducherry (one of the Navagraha sthala temples in Tamil Nadu, the principal Shani sthala); Shani Dham at Asola, Delhi; Shani Mandir at Indore; Shani temple at Mandapalli in Andhra Pradesh; Kuchanur Shaneeswarar temple in Tamil Nadu; the Shani shrine within the Surya temple at Konark, Odisha; and Shani Mahatmya temples at Mehandipur Balaji and Tirupati. Visiting any of these on Saturday during Sade Sati is considered exceptionally beneficial. (Sources: Wikipedia Shani temples; Hindupedia Navagraha sthalas; Shani Shingnapur temple guide.)
